assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 101 contains '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both additive and reductive signals present...' and the dedicated section at line 26 states 'Test Change Classification: MIXED' with line 28 stating 'This PR contains both additive and reductive test changes.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Report lines 32-38 explicitly identify 'Removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers' as the first reductive signal, describing that 'the entire test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers function was deleted' and detailing its previous behavior.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: Report lines 40-55 identify 'Relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic' as the second reductive signal, showing the base-branch assertion checked a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers ('pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar') while the PR-branch checks only the versioned PURL ('pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12').

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: Report lines 61-64 identify additive signal #2: 'New test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs (created): Contains three entirely new test functions: test_simplified_purl_no_version, test_simplified_purl_mixed_types, test_simplified_purl_ordering_preserved.'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Report lines 59-60 identify additive signal #1: 'New test function test_recommend_purls_dedup (added to tests/api/purl_recommend.rs): This function tests deduplication behavior that did not exist in the base branch.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all documentation/report files. No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified. The report is purely analytical.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary of findings. There is no merge action taken, no gh pr merge command, and no indication of auto-merging. The report presents its analysis for human review.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: The report does not attribute the test change classification to a 'Style/Conventions' domain or sub-agent. The classification appears as a standalone section 'Test Change Classification: MIXED' (line 26) and in the verification checks table (line 101), but neither location references 'Style/Conventions'. No mention of 'Style/Conventions' appears anywhere in the report or criterion files.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: Line 28 states 'The classification is based on structural comparison of the base-branch and PR-branch test file content.' Lines 32-38 describe comparing 'the base-branch file content (lines 30-48) against the PR diff.' Lines 40-55 compare 'base-branch assertion against the PR-branch assertion' with actual code snippets. Line 69 reiterates 'reductive signals identified by comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content.' The analysis references test function signatures and assertion values, not acceptance criteria.
